programme.title: Like a Version
programme.desc: Gigi Perez covers Lana Del Rey's 'Summertime Sadness' for Like A Version.

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Award-winning host Stephen Fry wrangles a stellar panel of comedians in the world's most impossible quiz show. This episode features Jimmy Carr, John Sergeant, Alan Davies and Jo Brand.

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.sub-title: Trinny Woodall, Reece Shearsmith, Michael Ball & Charlie Brooker
programme.desc: Trinny Woodall, Reece Shearsmith, Michael Ball and Charlie Brooker join David Mitchell and Lee Mack to hoodwink their opponents with facts and plausible lies about themselves.

programme.title: MythBusters
programme.desc: Could Jack have survived with Rose in the ending of Titanic? Is it possible to build a rocket-powered surfboard?

programme.title: Phil Spencer: Secret Agent
programme.desc: Phil travels to the Glasgow suburb of Giffnock to try to attract some buyers to a bungalow that's been in the same family for 37 years.

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: A GP and his wife decide to build a new home at the bottom of their garden. Problem is, right from the start they can't agree on any aspect of the design, even the basic shape.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Richard Osman hosts as lexicographer Susie Dent, newsreader Nick Owen, alpine ski racer Chemmy Alcott and comedian Dane Baptiste test their general knowledge skills.

programme.title: Spicks and Specks
programme.sub-title: Vika Bull, David O'Doherty, Anthony Callea & Stephen K Amos
programme.desc: Joining Adam, Myf and Alan tonight are Vika Bull, David O'Doherty, Anthony Callea and Stephen K Amos.

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Join Sandi Toksvig for a look at some \.

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon, Lee Mack and David Mitchell return for an episode packed with some of the best bits from the series. Among the guests are Jo Brand, Emma Bunton, Daisy May Cooper, Stacey Dooley, Rhod Gilbert and others.

programme.title: Tonight At The Museum
programme.desc: When the doors shut, the comedy begins: Alex Lee is joined by Colin Lane, Lizzy Hoo, Dane Simpson and Katie Robertson chasing display glory.

programme.title: Gruen
programme.desc: We look at the ad that made you scream 'crikey' and ask if the new Bankwest ads are the Bankworst. Plus our first ever audience suggested pitch. Wil, Todd and Russel are joined by Karen Ferry and Camey O'Keefe.

programme.title: The Weekly with Charlie Pickering
programme.desc: In the season finale of The Weekly, Charlie is joined by the stars of the new ABC comedy Bad Company, Kitty Flanagan and Anne Edmonds, plus Rhys Nicholson, Margaret Pomeranz and Alexei Toliopoulos.

programme.title: Planet America
programme.desc: Insane, demented or just Donald? Dr Mary Trump discusses her uncle's erratic behaviour and extreme comments. New tensions threaten the Iran ceasefire; a disgraced former presidential candidate resigns over sex scandal.

programme.title: ER
programme.desc: Carter tends to the sickly Dr. McNulty before stunning Abby by deciding to go to Africa. Kerry's former patient, alderman John Bright, returns bearing financial gifts.

programme.title: W1A
programme.desc: It is the year of charter renewal and a critical time for the BBC. The renewal group, under head of values Ian Fletcher, is tasked with identifying what the BBC does best and finding more ways of doing less of it, better.

programme.title: rage
programme.desc: Count down the hottest chart hits and catch up on some of the most promising new popular music videos on rage.

programme.title: The Weekly with Charlie Pickering
programme.desc: Why is our vaccine rollout such a clusterpfizer? Who pulled the handbrake on electric cars? What is Frank Woodley doing eating bugs? And is Tony Armstrong gorgeous enough to make bad news sound good?

programme.title: The Weekly with Charlie Pickering
programme.desc: Why our vaccine didn't roll out, Australia Post drama (unrelated), the unexpected loss of Prince Philip before his time and a bombshell in a lunch box. Plus, Margaret Pomeranz watches smut and Nat Tran beats the stock market.

programme.title: The Weekly with Charlie Pickering
programme.desc: The New Zealand Travel Bubble opens, Scott Morrison drops it like it's hot, milkshakes and tacos confusing the birds and the bees. Plus, Nath Valvo in your TV, and Kamahl takes Charlie on the trip of a lifetime.

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Join Sandi Toksvig and the QI team in search of Victory with Rob Beckett, Daliso Chaponda, Lara Ricote, and Alan Davies.

programme.title: If You're Listening
programme.desc: For years, many feared the weight of a colossal population. But in 2026, we've found ourselves in a world where we're not scared about having too many babies, but too few.

programme.title: ABC News Update
programme.desc: The news headlines updating the day's top stories from across Australia and around the world.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Richard Osman hosts as lexicographer Susie Dent, newsreader Nick Owen, alpine ski racer Chemmy Alcott and comedian Dane Baptiste test their general knowledge skills.

programme.title: Operation Ouch!
programme.desc: The Doctors explain how babies are made. Celebrating families of all kinds, they explain puberty, genital body parts, the role of consent, and how humans all start life when two special cells meet: a sperm and an egg.

programme.title: Operation Ouch!
programme.desc: Dr Chris boards a weightless flight and Dr Xand meets an astronaut to explore what happens to the human body in space, and find out how one sneezes, poops and wees in zero gravity.

programme.title: Malory Towers
programme.desc: Clarissa receives a letter from her father, he is writing to inform her that he has sold her pony, Merrylegs, because Clarissa isn't well enough to ride anymore. Clarissa is devastated, she didn't even get to say goodbye!

programme.title: Malory Towers
programme.desc: In the Secret Room, Felicity and June discover half of a map hidden in a secret compartment under the floorboards. It must be a clue as to the whereabouts of the hidden treasure.

programme.title: Total DramaRama
programme.desc: Beth and Leshawna try to one-up each other with fake injuries as they compete for the attention and sympathy of the other kids at daycare.

programme.title: Scooby-Doo and Guess Who?
programme.desc: Bill Nye helps a trapped Neil Degrasse Tyson.

programme.title: The Powerpuff Girls
programme.desc: The girls must enter a cursed jungle temple to obtain the last Heart Stone.

programme.title: Open Season: Call of Nature
programme.desc: When Elliot hears that there's a 'Golden Woo Hoo' ticket hidden not too far from Camp Wild, he proposes to Boog that they go on an adventure together to find it.

programme.title: Summer Camp Island
programme.desc: Hedgehog and Oscar find themselves trapped in Susie's cabin and must escape undetected.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Teri teaches Gumball and Darwin that germs really can affect them.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Robin takes the Titans on an educational trip to the Grand Canyon.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: A giant cocoon mysteriously appears in the Titans' living room.

programme.title: Miraculous: Tales of Ladybug & Cat Noir
programme.desc: Ladybug and Cat Noir must face a new enemy - mysterious, worrying, elusive, and yet close to them. Very close, but they have no idea.

programme.title: Unikitty
programme.desc: Dr. Fox is proud to unveil her new ultimate combat machine and take it into an underground scientist fighting ring. But Hawkodile's jealous of Dr. Fox's fighting bot that's supposedly a better puncher than him.

programme.title: Pokemon Horizons: The Series
programme.desc: The Rising Volt Tacklers locate Entei of the Six Heroes, but something seems off...

programme.title: Pokemon Horizons: The Series
programme.desc: The time has finally come - the Rising Volt Tacklers get a rematch with the black Rayquaza!

programme.title: Good Game: Spawn Squad
programme.desc: With 400-plus hours spent making his island in Animal Crossing, Luca impresses Gem with his glorious tropical escape and shares some pro tips.

programme.title: Let's Dance
programme.desc: Dancers learn how to dance country and perform it in the Wild, Wild West.

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: The Numberblocks explore a mysterious pyramid full of bar charts. They must read the clues in the charts to discover the pyramid's secrets - and to find their way out again!

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: Alphablocks O and G search for T. A DOG helps, but they lose it. They run on a LOG, then make a BOG to attract the dog. The dog finds T lost in a FOG. They make a POT and have tea.

programme.title: Let's Sing!
programme.desc: Join Tim Minchin and The Sydney Children's Choir and sing along to Tim's song, When I Grow Up.

programme.title: Bitesize Daily
programme.desc: Learn about experimenting with colour, making collages and drawing abstract landscapes.

programme.title: The Brilliant World of Tom Gates
programme.desc: When a pesky singing cat keeps Tom awake at night, he falls asleep in Mr Fullerman's class and drools in front of Amy; Tom shows how to make a pesky cat out of clay.

programme.title: BTN Specials
programme.desc: We meet students bringing back letter writing, a school running its own shop to learn about business skills, and one that's teaching their community about their school's history and its links to a famous Australian artist.

programme.title: Media Explained: Misinformation
programme.desc: Information and media are essential for our everyday lives. But with so much content out there, especially online, how do we know what to trust? Having a critical mindset can help us make informed decisions.

programme.title: Media Stamped
programme.desc: Is \.

programme.title: Move It Mobstyle
programme.desc: If you say you can, you can. If you say you can't, you can't. So repeat after dancers Shari Togo and Tahlia Burchill and say you can. Now get up and learn the choreography to The Last Kinection's beats.

programme.title: BtN Newsbreak
programme.desc: Find out what's going on in the world with a news bulletin designed for children, featuring stories that are relevant and easy to understand.

programme.title: Phil Spencer: Secret Agent
programme.desc: Phil travels to the Glasgow suburb of Giffnock to try to attract some buyers to a bungalow that's been in the same family for 37 years.

programme.title: W1A
programme.desc: Cross-dressing ex-Premier League footballer Ryan Chelford's appearance on a late-night, midweek edition of Match of the Day did not go well. Host Gary Lineker and pundit Alan Shearer were literally lost for words.

programme.title: Whose Line Is It Anyway?
programme.desc: Aisha Tyler is joined by regulars Wayne Brady, Ryan Stiles, and Colin Mochrie, along with guest performer Jonathan Mangum, and star guest from CW's Legacies, Chris Lee.

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Carmody gets an opportunity he shouldn't turn down but is happy with the way things are. Mrs Hall and Mr Bosworth clash over a Warden task. Both Siegfriedand Carmody are forced to pay attention to their romantic pursuits.

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The team attempt to investigate the mysterious death of a British tourist. On the surface, it looks like a clear-cut accident, but they realise nothing is quite what it seems in this case.

programme.title: ER
programme.desc: Carter tends to the sickly Dr. McNulty before stunning Abby by deciding to go to Africa. Kerry's former patient, alderman John Bright, returns bearing financial gifts.

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Welcome to a 'Hanatomy' lesson where the panel are discussing heads, hands, hips, hearts and other parts of the body beginning with H. Joining Stephen Fry are Alan Davies, Bill Bailey, Gyles Brandreth and Sue Perkins.

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.sub-title: Jo Brand, Simon Day, Kiri Pritchard-McClean & Henning Wehn
programme.desc: Guests Jo Brand, Simon Day, Kiri Pritchard-McClean and Henning Wehn join Lee Mack and David Mitchell.

programme.title: MythBusters
programme.desc: Can certain sound frequencies persuade people that an area is haunted? Is it really possible to smell fear? Is it really as easy to move and bury a dead body as it is in the movies?

programme.title: Phil Spencer: Secret Agent
programme.desc: Phil helps divorcees Sylvia and Bill sell their semi that's been loitering on the market for over a year. After 54 years of marriage they've decided to separate, sell their home, and move on.

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Farmer and architect come up with a house design which have never been seen before in his native Northern Ireland. The house is to be built of shipping containers and cantilevered over the top of a stream.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Richard Osman hosts as lexicographer Susie Dent, newsreader Nick Owen, alpine ski racer Chemmy Alcott and comedian Dane Baptiste test their general knowledge skills.

programme.title: Spicks and Specks
programme.sub-title: David Hobson, Cal Wilson, Carl Cox & Colin Lane
programme.desc: Joining Adam, Myf and Alan are David Hobson, Cal Wilson, Carl Cox and Colin Lane.

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Stephen Fry looks at everything in the kitchen but the sink, with Victoria Wood, Richard Osman, Jason Manford and Alan Davies.

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.sub-title: Chizzy Akudolu, Simon Gregson, Michelle Visage & Henning Wehn
programme.desc: Rob Brydon is in the host's chair, with Lee Mack and David Mitchell as the team captains.

programme.title: Cast Away
programme.desc: An obsessive executive for a worldwide delivery company finds himself stranded on a desert island after his flight home for Christmas ends in disaster.

programme.title: ER
programme.desc: A downed plane over the Great Lakes sends Abby's world into a tailspin. Susan considers a blind date while she works a case where an unpopular student ran down her classmates.

programme.title: W1A
programme.desc: Cross-dressing ex-Premier League footballer Ryan Chelford's appearance on a late-night, midweek edition of Match of the Day did not go well. Host Gary Lineker and pundit Alan Shearer were literally lost for words.

programme.title: Like a Version
programme.desc: triple j's legendary Like A Version segment hosts the greatest local and international acts, performing a cover of a song they love, and one of their own.

programme.title: rage
programme.desc: Australia's longest-running music show is home to the biggest vault of music videos from a diverse range of artists, genres, and eras. They curate the best clips of all-time to entertain for hours.

programme.title: The Set
programme.sub-title: Julia Stone, Kwame and Tasman Keith
programme.desc: Julia Stone, Kwame and Tasman Keith play their hits including Fire in Me and One, plus a one-off collab in front of a live crowd.

programme.title: The Set
programme.desc: The Jungle Giants, Sycco and Patience from the Grates play their hits, including Used to Be in Love and Dribble, plus special one-off collabs in front of a live crowd.

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig delves into the topic of Questions and Qualifications, with regular panellist Alan Davies, and guest players Ade Adepitan, Nish Kumar, and Holly Walsh.

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Welcome to a 'Hanatomy' lesson where the panel are discussing heads, hands, hips, hearts and other parts of the body beginning with H. Joining Stephen Fry are Alan Davies, Bill Bailey, Gyles Brandreth and Sue Perkins.

programme.title: ABC News Update
programme.desc: The news headlines updating the day's top stories from across Australia and around the world.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Richard Osman hosts as lexicographer Susie Dent, newsreader Nick Owen, alpine ski racer Chemmy Alcott and comedian Dane Baptiste test their general knowledge skills.

programme.title: Operation Ouch!
programme.desc: The doctors are talking about cancer and answering all the big questions. They explain what it is, why people get it, how it is treated, and how to help someone going through it.

programme.title: Malory Towers
programme.desc: The Fourth Form girls are full of nerves, it's the day of the School Cert Examination. Darrell knows how important it is that she pass if she wants to become a doctor one day.

programme.title: Malory Towers
programme.desc: Clarissa invites Ruth to come riding with her ,but as usual, Connie jumps in and speaks on behalf of her sister and invites herself along too. June can't help but have a go at Connie, chiding her for dominating her sister!

programme.title: Malory Towers
programme.desc: It's the day of Mam'zelle and Mr Edwards wedding and everyone is preparing for the big event. But Darrell and Felicity are concerned for June's whereabouts and set off in search of her.

programme.title: Total DramaRama
programme.sub-title: Stay Goth, Poodle Girl, Stay Goth
programme.desc: Gwen accidentally brings a pink stuffed pony she loves to daycare and spends the day denying it's hers.

programme.title: Scooby-Doo and Guess Who?
programme.sub-title: Now You Sia, Now You Don't!
programme.desc: The gang track a ghostly doppelganger that looks like Sia.

programme.title: Lego Ninjago Dragons Rising
programme.desc: When a pilotless Bounty flies into the Monastery, Lloyd, Arin, Sora, and Riyu follow clues back to Kai and Nya who are caught between warring realms.

programme.title: Transformers: Earthspark
programme.desc: Optimus Prime suspects GHOST is hiding something, so he sends Bumblebee on a secret recon mission deep inside GHOST HQ. The young Maltos sneak in and assist their recently injured mentor.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball and Darwin race home to spend quality time with Anais, but every possible obstacle gets in the way.

programme.title: Summer Camp Island
programme.desc: Oscar receives a camcorder from his parents and sets out to prove to them the magic on the island is real.

programme.title: 100% Wolf: Legend of the Moonstone
programme.desc: The kids try to track down Ivan, who's run away from school in possession of the moonstone, while also attending their first day of work placement.

programme.title: Caper Crew
programme.desc: When Ophelbert's beloved Guinea Pig is confiscated by scary canteen lady Gretchen, the kids plan a breakout from the local pound, with some much-needed help from Queenie.

programme.title: Be Cool, Scooby-Doo!
programme.desc: On a trip to Japan to visit Velma's friend, the gang encounters the ancient crab monster, Kaniaku, and attempts to solve a 700 year-old mystery. Meanwhile, Daphne becomes a viral media sensation.

programme.title: The Powerpuff Girls
programme.desc: The Powerpuff Girls compete in a race against some of their most vicious villains to win.

programme.title: Good Game: Spawn Squad
programme.desc: Four epic challenges, two amazing gamers and one winner! George and Harry go head-to-head in Minecraft, racing through underground, sticky, underwater and griefing mazes!

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.sub-title: Justice League's Next Top Talent Idol Star: Dance Crew Edition Part 1
programme.desc: The Justice League holds yet another talent competition, only this time they're facing off against the Titans.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.sub-title: Justice League's Next Top Talent Idol Star: Dance Crew Edition Part 2
programme.desc: In order to be the most talented superhero team in the DC universe, Robin and Beast Boy must work together on a jazz performance.

programme.title: Mechamato
programme.desc: A bad barber robot named Barberosak is causing havoc by giving bad haircuts to the people around town, including Amato.

programme.title: Lego Ninjago Dragons Rising
programme.desc: Kai, Sora and Wyldfyre infiltrate the Shadow Dojo as Ras begins the Blood Moon Ritual while Cole and Zane try to escape the Sorceress's spell vault.

programme.title: Pokemon: Ruby and Sapphire
programme.desc: Max finds an injured Ralts and tries to get it to a Pokemon Center, but its friends Gardevoir and Kirlia think our heroes are trying to steal it!

programme.title: Pokemon: Ruby and Sapphire
programme.desc: Ash gets ready for his final Gym battle in the Hoenn region: a Double Battle against Gym Leader Juan, who used to be a Poke;mon Coordinator!

programme.title: Phil Spencer: Secret Agent
programme.desc: Phil helps divorcees Sylvia and Bill sell their semi that's been loitering on the market for over a year. After 54 years of marriage they've decided to separate, sell their home, and move on.

programme.title: W1A
programme.desc: The search for new ways of saving money at the BBC continues - particularly important in the light of charter renewal.

programme.title: Semi-Pro
programme.desc: In 1970s Michigan, the age of big hair and short shorts, one man goes all the way in his search for glory and redemption.

programme.title: Whose Line Is It Anyway?
programme.desc: Aisha Tyler hosts another episode of the legendary improv show featuring regulars Wayne Brady, Ryan Stiles and Colin Mochrie along with guest performer Jeff Davis and star guest actor and singer Tinashe.

programme.title: ER
programme.desc: A downed plane over the Great Lakes sends Abby's world into a tailspin. Susan considers a blind date while she works a case where an unpopular student ran down her classmates.

programme.title: A Bite to Eat with Alice
programme.desc: Leading lady Lucy Durack arrives with one of her favourite ingredients, bananas. She also has a big challenge for Alice, how can she get her kids to eat more vegetables?

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: The panellists in this episode are willing and able to make a noise, and they are Alan Davies, Australia's Julia Morris, Rich Hall and Peter Serafinowicz.
